evals.prompt: Summarize our current Covent website/funnel strategy in elon-es style. Context: we have buyer search, free trial, weak marketing copy, some organic pull, churn risk, and possible buyer-data API upside.
evals.expected_output: Blunt first-principles bullet summary that identifies buyer data as the core asset, removes funnel friction, calls out churn risk, and ends with a north star.

evals.prompt: Give me the Elon-style executive summary of this product decision: should we add more CRM features or focus on proprietary buyer intelligence? Users are wholesalers, small users need workflow, bigger customers ask about integrations and data exports.
evals.expected_output: Bullet summary that contrasts surface features with core leverage, segments small vs big customers, and recommends focus without generic SaaS fluff.

evals.prompt: elon-es this: our onboarding has pricing page, feature page, demo CTA, then trial signup. Trial users are impatient and only convert when they see useful data immediately.
evals.expected_output: Short brutal funnel summary focused on immediate value, fewer steps, gated useful intelligence, and conversion/churn risk.
